faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Team@Once. Can't find the answer? Contact our support team.

escrow.question: How does the 14-day escrow protection work?
escrow.answer: When you fund a milestone, Stripe authorizes the payment but doesn't capture it yet. The developer sees 'Funded' status and begins work. After submission, you have 14 days to review. If you approve, payment is captured and released (minus 10% platform fee). If you do nothing, payment auto-releases after 14 days - protecting developers from clients who disappear.

dispute.question: What is the 7-2-2 dispute resolution system?
dispute.answer: If a dispute occurs: 7 days for negotiation where both parties submit evidence, 2 days for admin mediation where we review and decide the split, and 2 days for execution. This automated timeline ensures fast, fair resolutions without endless back-and-forth. Evidence is required - we don't rely on he-said-she-said.

templates.question: What are the 95+ project templates?
templates.answer: We provide pre-built project templates across 14 categories: Business Apps ($15k-$80k), E-Commerce ($10k-$50k), Healthcare ($20k-$100k), AI/ML ($25k-$150k), and more. Each template includes default milestones, tech stack recommendations, timeline estimates, and requirement checklists - so you start with a solid foundation.

team.question: How does team management work?
team.answer: We support three account types: Solo Developer (individual freelancer), Small Team (2-10 members), and Development Company (full agency). Team features include member roles (Owner, Admin, Developer, Designer, QA), workload tracking (visual capacity indicators), project assignments with allocation percentages, and performance metrics.

communication.question: What communication tools are included?
communication.answer: Our Communication Hub includes: Real-time chat with file attachments and reactions, video conferencing with screen sharing and recording, collaborative whiteboard for diagrams, meeting scheduler with timezone support, and project calendar for deadline tracking. All built-in - no need for external tools.

bidding.question: How does the bidding system work?
bidding.answer: Clients post projects without assigning a team. Developers browse the marketplace and submit proposals with cover letters, proposed costs, custom milestones, and team composition. AI calculates match scores based on tech stack overlap. Clients compare proposals and select a winner - other proposals are auto-rejected and the project leaves the marketplace.

proofOfDelivery.question: What proof-of-delivery is required?
proofOfDelivery.answer: Developers must upload deliverables (code, files, screenshots) when submitting milestones. The system tracks submission timestamps, resubmission counts, and review history. This creates an evidence trail that protects both parties in case of disputes - no more 'I delivered it' vs 'I never received it' arguments.

fees.question: What fees does Team@Once charge?
fees.answer: We charge 10% platform fee on successful deliveries only. This is deducted when payment is released to the developer. No hidden fees, no subscription costs, no upfront charges. If a milestone is refunded due to non-delivery, neither party pays any fee. We only make money when projects succeed.
